_comment: Source of truth for Get-Tools.ps1. URLs point to official upstream releases only. 'redistributable' indicates whether the license permits bundling the binary in this zip (it does NOT for several free-but-proprietary tools, which is why this toolkit downloads instead of bundling). Verify hashes/signatures yourself for evidentiary work.

categories.live_triage: Volatile-state and artifact collection on a running host

tools.name: Velociraptor
tools.notes: Swiss-army knife for endpoint triage; offline collector builds a single self-contained .exe. Preferred primary triage engine.

tools.name: KAPE
tools.notes: Targets+Modules artifact collection & parsing. Requires a free email registration to download. Cannot be auto-downloaded headlessly; script prints instructions.

tools.name: EricZimmermanTools
tools.license: Free per-tool, NON-redistributable as a bundle policy
tools.notes: MFTECmd, PECmd, AmcacheParser, AppCompatCacheParser, RegistryExplorer/RECmd, JLECmd, LECmd, SBECmd, EvtxECmd, Timeline Explorer, SrumECmd, WxTCmd, RBCmd. Get-ZimmermanTools.ps1 self-updates them.

tools.name: WinPmem
tools.notes: Physical-memory acquisition, x86/x64 ONLY (no ARM64 driver -- use DumpIt on ARM64). Run elevated. Capture to EXTERNAL media, never the suspect disk. Uses the Go userland build (go-winpmem) with signed drivers, invoked as 'acquire '; the legacy winpmem_mini_x64_rc2.exe writes a 0-byte image on modern Win10/11 (Velocidex issue #55) -- do NOT use it.

tools.name: DumpIt
tools.notes: PREFERRED memory acquirer -- the only free tool covering x86, x64 AND ARM64, each with a signed kernel driver. Writes a Microsoft crash dump (.dmp, WinDbg + Volatility 3 readable); CLI 'DumpIt.exe /O <file> /Q'. Free but email-registration-gated, so it cannot be auto-downloaded; script prints instructions. Place the per-arch builds at tools\DumpIt\ARM64\, tools\DumpIt\x64\, tools\DumpIt\x86\ (or a single tools\DumpIt\DumpIt.exe).

tools.name: Volatility3
tools.notes: Memory image analysis. Needs Python 3.8+. 'pip install volatility3' also works.

tools.name: Hayabusa
tools.notes: Fast Sigma-based Windows EVTX timeline & detection. Ships with curated rules.

tools.name: Chainsaw
tools.notes: EVTX/MFT/registry hunting with Sigma + built-in detections.

tools.name: Loki
tools.notes: Simple IOC & YARA scanner (Florian Roth). Run loki-upgrader first to pull current signatures.

tools.name: YARA
tools.notes: Pattern-matching engine. Pair with rules in iocs/yara/.

tools.name: Sysinternals
tools.notes: Autoruns, Process Explorer, Procmon, Sigcheck, TCPView, Handle, PsList, ListDLLs, Strings, Sysmon. Downloadable directly; EULA forbids re-hosting.

tools.name: Sysmon
tools.notes: If not already deployed, install with a curated config (SwiftOnSecurity / Olaf Hartong) for richer telemetry going forward. NOT retroactive.

tools.name: CyberChef
tools.notes: Offline-capable decode/deobfuscate web app (single HTML). Great for base64/XOR/encoded PowerShell.

tools.name: DB-Browser-SQLite
tools.notes: Inspect browser history, app SQLite stores (e.g., WebCacheV01, History).

tools.name: Autopsy
tools.notes: Full GUI dead-disk forensic platform (Sleuth Kit front-end). Large; install on the analysis workstation, not the endpoint.

tools.name: FTKImager
tools.notes: Forensic disk/logical imaging (E01). Requires free registration. Script prints instructions.
